title: veronica austriaca var. macrodonta
intro: Veronica austriaca var. macrodonta, also known as Austrian Speedwell, is a perennial plant belonging to the Plantaginaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal medicine, Austrian Speedwell is often used to treat respiratory problems, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
